description: Fix 10 verified bugs found during a comprehensive audit of the entire OpenTabs platform: data corruption in network capture, session memory leaks, watch mode reliability, log follow race conditions, dead code, modifier key handling, token extraction, message validation, version comparison, and extension installation cleanup.

userStories.title: Fix atob corrupting non-ASCII response bodies in network capture
userStories.description: As a developer using browser_get_network_requests, I want response bodies with non-ASCII characters (Chinese, emoji, accented text) to be captured correctly so that I can inspect any page's network traffic without data corruption.
acceptanceCriteria: Line 202 of network-capture.ts uses TextDecoder to decode base64 content instead of bare atob
The decode pattern is: new TextDecoder().decode(Uint8Array.from(atob(res.body), c => c.charCodeAt(0)))
Text content that is not base64-encoded (res.base64Encoded === false) is still stored directly without any decoding
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/network-capture.ts line 202. Current code: `const body = res.base64Encoded ? atob(res.body) : res.body;`. The Chrome DevTools Protocol returns base64-encoded UTF-8 text for response bodies. atob() decodes to a Latin1 binary string, not Unicode text. Fix: `const body = res.base64Encoded ? new TextDecoder().decode(Uint8Array.from(atob(res.body), c => c.charCodeAt(0))) : res.body;`. This is the standard pattern for decoding base64 to UTF-8 in browser environments.

userStories.title: Fix MCP session leak when transport connect throws
userStories.description: As the MCP server, I want to clean up session objects when transport connection fails so that failed connection attempts do not cause unbounded memory growth.
acceptanceCriteria: Lines 290-293 in http-routes.ts are wrapped in a try/catch that calls removeSession() on error before re-throwing
sessionServers.push(sessionServer) still happens before connect() so the removeSession closure can find it
The catch block calls removeSession() then rethrows the error so the outer handler returns an appropriate error response
The existing removeSession() closure handles cleanup correctly (it already checks indexOf and splices)

userStories.title: Fix EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN to exclude root-level tsconfig files
userStories.description: As a user running opentabs setup, I want the installed extension directory to not contain tsconfig files so that the extension installation is clean and contains only runtime-necessary files.
acceptanceCriteria: EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN in platform/shared/src/index.ts matches root-level tsconfig files (e.g., rel path 'tsconfig.json', 'tsconfig.test.json')
The regex still matches tsconfig files in subdirectories (existing behavior preserved)
The regex still matches node_modules, src, .git at any level (existing behavior preserved)
New test cases in validation.test.ts verify root-level tsconfig exclusion: 'tsconfig.json' -> excluded, 'tsconfig.test.json' -> excluded
userStories.notes: File: platform/shared/src/index.ts line 250. Current regex: `/(?:^|[\/])(?:node_modules|src|\.git)(?:[\/]|$)|[\/]tsconfig[^/\\]*/`. The tsconfig part only matches with a leading path separator `[\\/]tsconfig`. For root-level files, `relative()` returns `tsconfig.json` with no leading separator. Fix: change the tsconfig part to `(?:^|[\\/])tsconfig[^/\\]*` so it also matches at string start. Full regex: `/(?:^|[\\/])(?:node_modules|src|\.git)(?:[\\/]|$)|(?:^|[\\/])tsconfig[^/\\]*/`. Add test cases in platform/shared/src/validation.test.ts in the EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN describe block for 'tsconfig.json' (root) and 'tsconfig.test.json' (root) both matching the pattern.

userStories.title: Fix opentabs build --watch silently dropping file changes during rebuild
userStories.description: As a plugin developer using watch mode, I want every file save to trigger a rebuild so that my latest changes are always reflected in the built output.
acceptanceCriteria: A pendingRebuild flag (or equivalent) is added that is set to true when a rebuild is requested while building is true
After the current build completes, the code checks pendingRebuild and initiates another rebuild if set
The pendingRebuild flag is cleared at the start of each rebuild
The existing debounce behavior is preserved (changes are still debounced before triggering rebuild)

userStories.title: Fix followFile race condition in opentabs logs
userStories.description: As a developer tailing server logs, I want the log follower to not duplicate or skip lines when the log file is being written to rapidly.
acceptanceCriteria: offset is updated in the stream's 'end' event callback instead of synchronously before the stream delivers data
A reading flag prevents concurrent readNewContent calls from creating overlapping streams
When readNewContent is called while a read is in flight, it is deferred until the current read completes
File truncation detection (offset > currentSize) still works correctly

userStories.title: Remove dead code in handleBrowserTypeText clear logic
userStories.description: As a code maintainer, I want the type-text handler to not contain dead code so that the clear-then-type logic is clear and unambiguous.
acceptanceCriteria: The redundant input.value = '' assignment (line 529) is removed from the func parameter in handleBrowserTypeText
The remaining input.value = clr ? txt : input.value + txt assignment correctly handles both clear and append modes
Existing E2E tests for browser_type_text still pass

userStories.title: Add separate meta modifier parameter to browser_press_key
userStories.description: As an AI agent automating keyboard input, I want to send Ctrl and Meta (Cmd) modifier keys independently so that web apps that distinguish between Ctrl+key and Cmd+key receive correct keyboard events.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_press_key tool input schema has a new optional 'meta' boolean parameter (default false) alongside the existing 'ctrl' parameter
The KeyboardEvent metaKey property is set from the new meta parameter, not from ctrl
The ctrlKey property continues to be set from the ctrl parameter
The tool description mentions the meta parameter for Cmd key on macOS
Existing E2E tests for browser_press_key still pass
userStories.notes:
Two files need changes:

1. MCP server tool definition: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/press-key.ts — add `meta: z.boolean().optional().default(false).describe('Meta (Cmd on macOS) modifier key')` to the input schema. Pass `meta: args.meta` in the dispatch params alongside existing `ctrl: args.ctrl`.

2. Extension handler: platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands.ts line 1244 — currently `metaKey: ctrl`. Change to read the `meta` parameter from params (with fallback to false) and use it: `const meta = params.meta === true;` then `metaKey: meta`. The existing ctrl parameter continues to set ctrlKey.

The handler reads params as Record<string, unknown>, so adding a new param is straightforward — just read `params.meta` with a boolean check.

userStories.title: Fix Slack token regex to include underscores in character class
userStories.description: As a Slack plugin user, I want the token extraction to match Slack tokens that contain underscores so that authentication works with all valid Slack token formats.
acceptanceCriteria: The regex on line 157 of slack-api.ts uses character class [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ instead of [a-zA-Z0-9-]+ for the xoxc- token match
The fix only changes the token character class, not the surrounding regex structure
userStories.notes: File: plugins/slack/src/slack-api.ts line 157. Current regex: `/["']?api_token["']?\s*:\s*["'](xoxc-[a-zA-Z0-9-]+)["']/`. The character class `[a-zA-Z0-9-]+` does not include underscores. Slack xoxc- tokens can contain underscores. Change to `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+`. This is a one-character fix. Build and type-check the plugin with `cd plugins/slack && bun run build`. Note: plugins are standalone projects (not in bun workspaces), so root build/lint/test commands don't cover them.

userStories.title: Add runtime object check for extension message params
userStories.description: As the MCP server, I want to validate that incoming WebSocket message params are actually objects before casting so that malformed messages from the extension don't cause silent misbehavior.
acceptanceCriteria: handleExtensionMessage in extension-protocol.ts validates that parsed.params is a plain object (or undefined/null) before processing
Non-object params values (numbers, strings, arrays, booleans) are rejected with a warning log
Messages with valid object params or no params continue to work exactly as before
The validation happens once before the method-specific handlers, not duplicated in each handler

userStories.title: Fix isNewer version comparison for NaN segments
userStories.description: As the MCP server checking for plugin updates, I want version comparison to handle malformed version strings correctly so that update notifications are accurate even for non-standard version formats.
acceptanceCriteria: isNewer correctly returns true for isNewer('1.0.abc', '2.0.0') (latest is newer despite malformed current)
isNewer correctly returns false for isNewer('2.0.0', '1.0.abc') (current is newer despite malformed latest)
isNewer still returns correct results for normal semver versions (existing behavior preserved)
isNewer still correctly handles prerelease suffixes with hyphens (existing behavior preserved)
New test cases cover the NaN segment scenarios
